target_goal.label: independent RedCube visual-deliverable domain agent with an OPL-hosted integration path
target_goal.summary: Freeze the target shape in which direct RedCube entry and the OPL-hosted path converge onto the same service-safe RedCube domain-agent entry, while OPL remains the product-managed adapter/projection layer over the configured family runtime provider, RedCube owns visual-domain truth, and Codex CLI remains the default concrete executor.

final_target_route.redcube_direct_entry: User -> RedCube Product Entry -> RedCube service-safe domain entry -> executor adapter -> concrete executor -> RedCube visual-domain truth surfaces
final_target_route.opl_hosted_entry: User -> OPL Product Entry -> OPL stage-led family runtime provider -> RedCube service-safe domain entry -> executor adapter -> concrete executor -> RedCube visual-domain truth surfaces

opl_runtime_manager.layer_role: product-managed adapter/projection layer over the configured family runtime provider

redcube_owns: domain-agent authority across gateway -> family -> profile -> pack boundaries
must_not_claim: repo-local managed runtime pilot is the long-term runtime owner
service-safe domain entry already equals a landed end-user product entry
RedCube AI became the whole OPL
RedCube AI is an OPL-internal workflow
repo-local Hermes wrapper proves external Hermes-Agent substrate ownership

repo_verified_today: OPL-hosted path targets the configured family runtime provider without owning RedCube visual-domain truth
RedCube service-safe domain entry is callable
RedCube product-entry service surface is callable
OPL-hosted handoff can enter the same downstream product entry
direct and oplHosted entries converge to the same downstream domain-agent entry
Codex CLI remains the default concrete executor behind executor adapter
not_landed_yet: managed web product shell on top of the landed service surface

excluded_scope: repackaging repo-local Hermes naming as upstream proof
claiming a mature end-user product shell has landed
